name: Cybersecurity Strategy
description: The organization shall define and maintain a cybersecurity strategy aligned with its business objectives and national cybersecurity requirements. The strategy must be reviewed and updated at least annually or following significant changes.

name: Cybersecurity Policy
description: A formal cybersecurity policy shall be established, approved by senior management, and communicated to all employees and relevant third parties. The policy must cover acceptable use, roles and responsibilities, and consequences of non-compliance.

name: Cybersecurity Roles and Responsibilities
description: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities shall be defined, documented, and assigned to qualified individuals including a designated Cybersecurity Officer. Accountability for cybersecurity decisions must be clearly established at all organizational levels.

name: Cybersecurity Risk Management
description: A cybersecurity risk management process shall be established to identify, assess, treat, and monitor cybersecurity risks to the organization's information assets. Risk assessments must be conducted at least annually and following material changes.

name: Cybersecurity Compliance Management
description: The organization shall identify and comply with applicable laws, regulations, and contractual obligations related to cybersecurity including NCA directives, PDPL, and sector-specific requirements. Compliance status must be tracked and reported to management.

name: Cybersecurity in Projects
description: Cybersecurity requirements shall be integrated into all project lifecycles, including new systems, infrastructure changes, and digital transformation initiatives. A cybersecurity review must be conducted before any new system is commissioned.

name: Cybersecurity Awareness and Training
description: All employees shall receive role-appropriate cybersecurity awareness training upon hiring and at least annually thereafter. Specialized technical training must be provided to staff with cybersecurity responsibilities.

name: Cybersecurity Periodic Review and Audit
description: Independent cybersecurity audits and reviews shall be conducted at least annually to assess compliance with policies, standards, and regulatory requirements. Audit findings must be tracked to remediation.

name: IT Asset Inventory
description: An accurate and up-to-date inventory of all IT assets (hardware, software, data) shall be maintained, including asset owner, classification, and location. The inventory must be reviewed at least quarterly.

name: OT Asset Management
description: An inventory of operational technology (OT) and industrial control systems assets shall be maintained separately from IT assets, capturing firmware versions, network interfaces, and communication protocols. OT asset changes must follow a dedicated change management process.

name: Identity Management
domain: Cybersecurity Defense — Identity and Access Management
description: A formal identity lifecycle management process shall govern the creation, modification, disabling, and deletion of user accounts across all systems. Each user must have a unique identity; shared accounts are prohibited except for documented exceptions.

name: Access Management
domain: Cybersecurity Defense — Identity and Access Management
description: Access to systems and information shall be granted on a least-privilege and need-to-know basis, with access rights reviewed at least every six months. Role-based access control (RBAC) must be implemented for all critical systems.

name: Privileged Access Management
domain: Cybersecurity Defense — Identity and Access Management
description: Privileged accounts (administrator, root, service accounts) shall be strictly controlled, monitored, and managed through a privileged access management (PAM) solution. Privileged sessions must be recorded and logs retained for a minimum of one year.

name: Information Classification
description: All information assets shall be classified according to their sensitivity level (e.g., public, internal, confidential, secret) and handled in accordance with the classification policy. Classification must be applied at the point of creation.

name: Information Handling and Labelling
description: Classified information shall be labelled and handled according to its classification level, with controls covering storage, transmission, printing, and sharing. Unauthorized disclosure or transfer of confidential information is prohibited.

name: Information Retention and Disposal
description: Information shall be retained for the period required by regulatory and business requirements and securely disposed of when no longer needed using approved methods (e.g., cryptographic erasure, physical destruction). Disposal must be documented.

name: Secure Endpoint Configuration
description: All endpoint devices (workstations, laptops, mobile devices) shall be configured according to approved security baselines with unnecessary services and ports disabled. Configuration compliance must be verified before deployment and monitored continuously.

name: Secure Server Configuration
description: Servers shall be hardened according to approved security baselines (e.g., CIS benchmarks) before deployment, with unnecessary services, ports, and protocols disabled. Configuration drift must be detected and remediated within defined timeframes.

name: Network Segmentation
description: Networks shall be segmented using firewalls, VLANs, and DMZ architectures to isolate critical systems, OT networks, and sensitive data from general-purpose networks. Internet-facing systems must be separated from internal systems.

name: Remote Access Security
description: All remote access to organizational systems shall require multi-factor authentication (MFA) and be conducted over encrypted channels (e.g., VPN with TLS 1.2 or higher). Remote access sessions must be logged and monitored.

name: Network Traffic Monitoring
description: Network traffic shall be monitored for anomalous activity, unauthorized connections, and indicators of compromise using network detection and response (NDR) or equivalent tools. Alerts must be reviewed by the security operations function.

name: Cybersecurity Event Log Management
description: Security-relevant event logs shall be collected from all critical systems, centralized in a SIEM or log management platform, and retained for a minimum of 12 months with at least 3 months online. Log integrity must be protected from tampering.

name: Security Monitoring and Detection
description: A 24x7 security monitoring capability shall be established to detect, correlate, and alert on cybersecurity events in near real-time. Detection rules must cover the MITRE ATT&CK techniques applicable to the organization's threat profile.

name: Vulnerability Assessment
description: Regular vulnerability assessments shall be conducted on all systems using approved scanning tools, with critical and high vulnerabilities remediated within 15 days and medium vulnerabilities within 30 days. Scan results must be tracked to closure.

name: Patch Management
description: A formal patch management process shall ensure that security patches are evaluated, tested, and applied within defined timeframes based on severity. Emergency patches for critical vulnerabilities must be applied within 72 hours.

name: Penetration Testing
description: Penetration testing shall be conducted at least annually on critical systems and applications by qualified internal teams or licensed third-party providers. Findings must be remediated and retested within agreed timelines.

name: Cryptographic Controls
description: Encryption shall be applied to sensitive data at rest and in transit using approved algorithms and key lengths (e.g., AES-256 for data at rest, TLS 1.2+ for data in transit). Cryptographic key management procedures must be documented and enforced.

name: Malware Protection
description: Anti-malware and endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions shall be deployed on all endpoints and servers with signatures updated at least daily. Malware detections must be automatically quarantined and reported to the security team.

name: Physical Access Control
domain: Cybersecurity Defense — Physical and Environmental Security
description: Physical access to data centers, server rooms, and areas housing critical systems shall be restricted to authorized personnel using multi-factor physical controls (e.g., badge + PIN or biometric). Access logs must be maintained and reviewed.

name: Environmental Controls
domain: Cybersecurity Defense — Physical and Environmental Security
description: Data centers and server rooms shall be protected against environmental threats including fire, flood, temperature extremes, and power failure using detection systems and redundant infrastructure (UPS, generators, CRAC units).

name: Secure Application Development
description: Secure development practices (SSDLC) shall be followed for all internally developed applications, including security requirements definition, threat modeling, secure coding guidelines, and security testing before release.

name: Application Security Testing
description: Web applications and APIs exposed to the internet or handling sensitive data shall undergo static application security testing (SAST) and dynamic application security testing (DAST) before deployment and after significant changes.

name: Email Security
domain: Cybersecurity Defense — Email and Web Security
description: Email security controls shall include anti-spam, anti-phishing, anti-malware scanning, DMARC/DKIM/SPF enforcement, and protection against business email compromise (BEC). Suspicious emails must be reported through a defined process.

name: Web Browsing Security
domain: Cybersecurity Defense — Email and Web Security
description: Web browsing from organizational devices shall be filtered using a web proxy or secure web gateway to block access to malicious, inappropriate, and unauthorized categories. SSL/TLS inspection must be applied to encrypted web traffic.

name: Data Loss Prevention
description: Data loss prevention (DLP) controls shall be implemented to detect and prevent unauthorized exfiltration of sensitive data via email, web, removable media, and cloud storage. DLP policies must be tuned to minimize false positives while maintaining coverage.

name: Cybersecurity Incident Management
description: A documented cybersecurity incident management process shall define roles, responsibilities, escalation paths, and response procedures for detecting, containing, eradicating, and recovering from cybersecurity incidents. Major incidents must be reported to NCA within the mandated timeframe.

name: Business Continuity Management
description: Business continuity plans (BCP) shall address cybersecurity scenarios including ransomware, system outages, and data breaches, with defined recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO). BCP must be tested at least annually.

name: Disaster Recovery
description: Disaster recovery plans (DRP) shall ensure that critical systems and data can be restored within defined RTO/RPO targets following a major cybersecurity incident or infrastructure failure. Recovery capabilities must be validated through regular drills.

name: Third-Party Cybersecurity Risk Management
description: Cybersecurity risks introduced by third-party vendors, suppliers, and service providers shall be assessed before engagement and monitored throughout the relationship. Third parties with access to sensitive systems or data must meet minimum cybersecurity standards.

name: Cloud Computing Security
description: Cloud services shall be assessed for compliance with NCA cloud cybersecurity controls before adoption, with shared responsibility boundaries clearly defined. Data residency, sovereignty, and encryption requirements must be contractually enforced.

name: Managed Security Services
description: Managed security service providers (MSSPs) shall be subject to contractual cybersecurity obligations including service levels for detection and response, data handling, and audit rights. MSSP access to systems must be logged and monitored.

name: ICS Asset Management and Inventory
description: An inventory of all industrial control system (ICS) and SCADA assets shall be maintained, including firmware versions, communication protocols, and network connectivity. Changes to ICS assets must follow a dedicated OT change management process.

name: ICS Security Architecture
description: ICS/OT networks shall be isolated from IT and corporate networks using air gaps, industrial firewalls, or data diodes, with strict controls on any cross-network communication. The Purdue Model or equivalent ICS segmentation architecture must be documented.

name: ICS Security Monitoring
description: ICS/OT environments shall be monitored for cybersecurity threats using passive monitoring tools designed for OT protocols (e.g., Modbus, DNP3, IEC 61850) without disrupting operational processes. Anomalies must trigger alerts to both cybersecurity and operations teams.
